summary: Created the missing lane-C task artifacts, extracted simulation result models into a dedicated owned module, added preparatory type-clean registry shim files without touching the concurrently claimed registry.py implementation, and verified the owned devtools and simulation production files with focused pytest and pyright proof.
known_risks: The registry shim files are not wired into registry.py yet because registry.py remains claimed by concurrent workstreams outside this lane's allowed scope.
Lane D must still move the validated uncovered files into tool.pyright.strict because this lane intentionally left pyproject.toml unchanged.
The repo venv required pyright[nodejs] so the native Windows pyright wrapper could run in this machine configuration.
